programme.title: Jurassic Ghost Town: A Mass Murder Mystery
programme.desc: An international team of paleontologists investigates a 150-million-year-old cold case; fifteen giant dinosaur skeletons lie buried in a remote corner of Wyoming's badlands; the team's mission is to discover why they were here and what killed them.

programme.title: The Loch Ness Monster Lives: A Mysteries at the Museum Special
programme.desc: Don Wildman goes on a quest to uncover the real story behind the centuries-old mystery of the Loch Ness Monster and to explore a few of the biggest hoaxes associated with the aquatic beast that locals call Nessie.

programme.title: Jurassic Ghost Town: A Mass Murder Mystery
programme.desc: An international team of paleontologists investigates a 150-million-year-old cold case; fifteen giant dinosaur skeletons lie buried in a remote corner of Wyoming's badlands; the team's mission is to discover why they were here and what killed them.

programme.title: The Loch Ness Monster Lives: A Mysteries at the Museum Special
programme.desc: Don Wildman goes on a quest to uncover the real story behind the centuries-old mystery of the Loch Ness Monster and to explore a few of the biggest hoaxes associated with the aquatic beast that locals call Nessie.

programme.title: Tales From the Explorers Club
programme.desc: Josh recounts a Himalayan mission to find the yeti and a filmmaker's pursuit of the dangerous elephant king; the starving crew of an Antarctic expedition makes difficult choices to survive.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Tales From the Explorers Club
programme.desc: Josh Gates explores daring journeys, including the Kon-Tiki balsa raft crossing the Pacific, NASA's lunar rover making tracks on the moon and an airplane flying around the world without the use of fuel.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Tales From the Explorers Club
programme.desc: Josh Gates recounts the death-defying exploits of Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay, who summit Mount Everest; two submariners plunge seven miles under the Pacific; a pilot skydives from the edge of space.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Killers of the Cosmos
programme.desc: More than 100 million pieces of small space debris are floating around the Earth; a collision between two of these objects in low Earth orbit could be catastrophic.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Killers of the Cosmos
programme.desc: Until recently, black holes were only theoretical; scientists know that millions exist and some of them have the potential to be lethal to earth.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Killers of the Cosmos
programme.desc: Aliens could be lethal killers who want to invade and plunder our world; a team of experts investigate extra-terrestrial beings and their threat to human life.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Galaxies are locked in continual combat across the universe, and the Milky Way will face its biggest opponent ever, the Andromeda Galaxy, in a titanic and deadly struggle that could create a new and unrecognizable super galaxy.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: The Rosetta mission is a groundbreaking expedition to land on a comet for the very first time; special access reveals what this cutting-edge journey discovers and how these mysterious objects help create life on Earth.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: On the island of Pohnpei in Micronesia, explorers discover ancient structures hidden beneath dense vegetation; a strange glowing forest is discovered in India; an immense tunnel system is investigated in the Brazilian jungle.

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: On the island of Madagascar, paleontologists discover an intact fossil unlike any known animal, living or extinct; bizarre stone spheres are discovered in Costa Rica; the curious remains of a small man are found in Chiapas, Mexico.

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: On the volcanic island of Kolombagara, explorers uncover a mysterious man-made cave in the dense jungle; a researcher finds two unknown animal skulls in Vietnam; human remains are discovered in a narrow chamber of a South African cave system.

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: In the rain forests of Belize, researchers find a bizarre skeleton deep underground in a mysterious cave; an ancient city is discovered on a remote plateau in Cambodia; a strange coin is unearthed on the Wessel Islands of Australia.

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: In the tropical lowlands of Northern Colombia, a team of scientists discover a massive, unidentified animal vertebra; dozens of jars packed with bones are uncovered in Cambodia; a strange rusted vehicle is found on the small island of Peleliu.

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: Off the coast of Tanzania, a puzzling site containing massive stone blocks is revealed on Kilwa Island; the discovery of giant animal bones baffle researchers in Australia; hundreds of human bones are uncovered in a Guatemalan rain forest.

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: In Guatemala, archaeologists identify an ancient site containing a series of unknown structures; a lake full of jellyfish is studied on a remote South Pacific island; Argentinian researchers discover a complex that may have a dark past.

programme.title: Secrets in the Sand
programme.desc: Scan the skeleton of a drowned Stone Age fisherman in Chile and rediscover a vanished golden age in Egypt's lost city of Aten; ancient Chinese mummies reignite a cultural debate; discover a massive biblical-era moat in Jerusalem.

programme.title: Secrets in the Sand
programme.sub-title: Forgotten Mongol Soldiers and an Ancient Iraqi Tavern
programme.desc: Inspect a Mongolian mass grave and learn the fate of a platoon of soldiers, then investigate a 5,000-year-old tavern in Iraq; examine an Egyptian scarab that's far from home and ancient tools that show where desert migration routes once lay.

programme.title: Secrets in the Sand
programme.desc: Experts uncover four global stories of extraordinary curiosities that were once concealed in the deserts of the planet.

programme.title: Secrets in the Sand
programme.desc: Dig up a mysterious WW II bomber in the Libyan desert, then see why the carved pillars in a Turkish temple may be the world's oldest calendar; open a secret filled shrine in Jerusalem and unearth broken Iraqi bowls that have deep political meaning.

programme.title: Secrets in the Sand
programme.desc: Examine a tiny skeleton in Chile that set off a firestorm of alien speculation; explore a forgotten Silk Road settlement; uncover evidence of a mass sacrifice in northern Peru and visit a multi-faceted military site from the age of the pharaohs.

programme.title: Secrets in the Sand
programme.desc: Experts uncover four global stories of extraordinary curiosities that were once concealed in the deserts of the planet.

programme.title: Secrets in the Sand
programme.desc: Dig up a mysterious WW II bomber in the Libyan desert, then see why the carved pillars in a Turkish temple may be the world's oldest calendar; open a secret filled shrine in Jerusalem and unearth broken Iraqi bowls that have deep political meaning.

programme.title: Secrets in the Sand
programme.desc: Examine a tiny skeleton in Chile that set off a firestorm of alien speculation; explore a forgotten Silk Road settlement; uncover evidence of a mass sacrifice in northern Peru and visit a multi-faceted military site from the age of the pharaohs.

programme.title: Secrets in the Sand
programme.desc: Experts uncover four global stories of extraordinary curiosities that were once concealed in the deserts of the planet.

programme.title: Secrets in the Sand
programme.desc: Experts uncover four global stories of extraordinary curiosities that were once concealed in the deserts of the planet.

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: Off the coast of Tanzania, a puzzling site containing massive stone blocks is revealed on Kilwa Island; the discovery of giant animal bones baffle researchers in Australia; hundreds of human bones are uncovered in a Guatemalan rain forest.

programme.title: Secrets in the Jungle
programme.desc: In Guatemala, archaeologists identify an ancient site containing a series of unknown structures; a lake full of jellyfish is studied on a remote South Pacific island; Argentinian researchers discover a complex that may have a dark past.

programme.title: Killers of the Cosmos
programme.desc: Earth is hit by 100 tons of rock from space every day; most burn up in the atmosphere, but a single rock wiped out the dinosaurs.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Killers of the Cosmos
programme.desc: When a massive star dies, it can direct a blast of radiation that could obliterate all life.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Killers of the Cosmos
programme.desc: Experts discuss the many possible ways that could spark the beginning of the end for the universe, including when and why it would happen.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.sub-title: Birth of the Monster Black Holes
programme.desc: New discoveries reveal an astonishing supermassive black hole that was born during the earliest days of the cosmos, and finding out how this giant grew so large so quickly may help to explain the very formation of the universe itself.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: A brand-new NASA mission to the sun reveals cutting-edge discoveries about Earth's star; as the Parker Solar Probe dives through the sun's atmosphere, its findings challenge everything known about this strange place.

rating.system: Departamento de Justiça, Classificação, Títulos e Qualificação

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.desc: A museum in South London contains a disturbing tattered hat made of brown felt and decorated with more than 80 human teeth; using the latest imaging technology, experts investigate to uncover the macabre origins of this mysterious Victorian-era cap.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.desc: Discovered in the ruins of the ancient city of Ur, a mysterious wooden box adorned with an elaborate mosaic continues to mystify archaeologists today; experts explore the meaning behind its intricate and enigmatic images.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.desc: In 1953, a team of archaeologists uncover a bizarre, plastered human skull in the ancient city of Jericho; experts investigate the mysterious purpose and identity behind this 9,000-year-old clay-covered artifact.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.desc: A minuscule metal sphere containing two microscopic holes could be the smallest murder weapon in history; experts explore how this tiny object is linked to one of the most bizarre true crime cases of the 20th century.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.desc: Investigating whether a machine can read personalities; an ancient golden mask painted with human blood; a cursed chicken pot.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.desc: The shattered skull of a 19th century railway worker is now one of the most astonishing objects in the history of medical science; experts explore this traumatic injury to reveal how it changed the field of neuroscience.

programme.title: Strangest Things
programme.desc: Containing intricately carved concentric circles, the Towie Ball is one of the finest Neolithic stoneworks ever discovered; using cutting-edge imaging technology, experts investigate the purpose behind this 5,000-year-old black stone mystery.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: In Egypt, a freakish animal is caught on camera, and using cutting-edge tech, experts investigate if this strange beast could explain the secrets of a satanic deity that was once believed to have been worshiped here.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: When a strange, dried-out creature washes ashore down river from a nuclear power plant, some experts suspect it's the mummified corpse of a mutant; using the latest science and technology, this incredible theory is put to the test.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Cameras capture footage of a highway swarmed with snake-like creatures and an unexplained ooze; the latest tech reveals where they came from and their top-secret purpose.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.sub-title: The Man Who Laid an Egg
programme.desc: When cameras capture footage of a man who appears to lay an egg, some experts wonder if it's evidence that scientists created a human-reptile mutant through gene manipulation; using cutting-edge tech, investigators put this theory to the test.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: When cameras record a strange creature in the wilderness perform an incredible feat of strength, some suspect it's hard proof of Bigfoot; using cutting-edge science, experts investigate if they've discovered the spawn of Sasquatch and Iron Man.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Cameras capture footage of a ghostly figure in the window of Pablo Escobar's abandoned luxury building, mere moments before it is demolished, and some believe it was the notorious drug lord himself.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.sub-title: The Man Who Laid an Egg
programme.desc: When cameras capture footage of a man who appears to lay an egg, some experts wonder if it's evidence that scientists created a human-reptile mutant through gene manipulation; using cutting-edge tech, investigators put this theory to the test.
